draft.introduction: The disciples ask who is greatest in the kingdom, and Jesus places a child in their midst. The reversal—greatness through humility—overturns both Roman and Jewish status hierarchies.

translation.text: At that time the disciples came to Jesus, saying, “Who is the greatest in the kingdom of heaven?” And calling to him a child, he put him in the midst of them and said, “Truly, I say to you, unless you turn and become like children, you will never enter the kingdom of heaven. Whoever humbles himself like this child is the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.”
translation.original_text: ἐὰν μὴ στραφῆτε καὶ γένησθε ὡς τὰ παιδία

translator_notes.note: “Become like children” (γένησθε ὡς τὰ παιδία) does not idealize childish innocence—children in the ancient world had no legal status. The point is that kingdom entry requires accepting the social position of those with no claim to honor.
